message:
🌱 Add kubernetes: Support the user flag from docker exec in kubectl exec mission (#1558)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,74 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "kubernetes-30656-support-the-user-flag-from-docker-exec-in-kubectl-exec",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "kubernetes: Support the user flag from docker exec in kubectl exec",
+    "description": "Support the user flag from docker exec in kubectl exec. Requested by 426+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current kubernetes deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your kubernetes version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kubernetes -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubernetes\nhelm list -n kubernetes 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working kubernetes installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review kubernetes configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant kubernetes configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n kubernetes -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubernetes\nkubectl get configmap -n kubernetes -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubernetes\n```\nIt looks like `docker exec` is being used as the backend for `kubectl exec`. `docker exec` has the `--user` flag, which allows you to run a command as a particular user.  This same functionality doesn't exist in Kubernetes."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Support the user flag from docker exec in kubectl exec",
+        "description": "considerations:\n* uid would need to be part of the exec interface for CRI, and all runtimes would need to support it\n* would need to figure out what the authorization check would be w.r.t. pod security policy restrictions on uids\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kubernetes -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubernetes\nkubectl get events -n kubernetes --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Support the user flag from docker exec in kubectl exec\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "considerations:\n* uid would need to be part of the exec interface for CRI, and all runtimes would need to support it\n* would need to figure out what the authorization check would be w.r.t. pod security policy restrictions on uids",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
